findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 28. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: Slide 2 'Our Role' has a two-column layout in the PDF (NC DEQ on left, SC DNR on right, with partner logos arranged in a grid below). The HTML renders all content as a single column, stacking all logos and screenshots vertically.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows DEQ website screenshot and SC DNR website screenshot side-by-side in two columns, with partner logos arranged in a multi-column grid below. HTML renders everything in a single vertical column.

findings.description: Slide 4 has a two-column layout in the PDF (reporting form on the left, key takeaways bullet list and agency logos on the right). The HTML renders these sequentially as a single column.
findings.evidence: PDF page 4 shows the Aquatic Plant Report form on the left and key takeaways + DEQ/Wildlife logos on the right side-by-side. HTML stacks them vertically.

findings.description: Slide 5 'How to Take a Good ID Photo' has a two-column layout with good photos (green border) on left and bad photos (red border) on right. The HTML collapses this into a single column, losing the clear good vs. bad comparison.
findings.evidence: PDF page 5 shows green-bordered good example photos on the left and red-bordered bad example photos on the right in a side-by-side comparison. HTML stacks them vertically, making the comparison less clear.

findings.description: Slide 6 'How to Get in Touch' has a two-column layout with contact info on left and 'Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers' poster on right. HTML stacks them vertically.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 shows contact emails on the left side and the Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers infographic on the right. HTML renders them in a single vertical column.

findings.description: The URL 'duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants' appears as plain text in the HTML in the section under 'New Reporting Tool' heading, without being wrapped in an <a href> tag. In the PDF it appears as a clickable link.
findings.evidence:
HTML: '

duke-energy.com/AquaticPlants

' under the 'New Reporting Tool' section. The same URL also appears as plain text in the bullet list under 'Key takeaways'. The PDF page 3 shows it as a hyperlink.

findings.description: The 'Related Links' section from PDF page 3, which lists several links including 'Aquatic Plant Reporting Form', 'Catawba-Wateree Aquatic Plant Survey', 'Hydrilla Fact Sheet', 'What to Know About Harmful Algal Blooms', and 'How Duke Energy's aquatic plant scientists survey lake shorelines', is missing from the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 shows a 'Related Links' sidebar with 5 linked items. The HTML screenshot section does not reproduce this text content.

findings.description: The descriptive body text from the PDF page 3 about aquatic plant management is missing from the HTML. This includes paragraphs about invasive species threatening well-being of systems, how plants were introduced, and Duke Energy's survey program.
findings.evidence: PDF page 3 contains: 'A key aspect of being a good environmental steward is monitoring and managing our rivers and lakes for aquatic plants and wildlife...' and 'Many invasive aquatic plants have the potential to invade Duke Energy's lakes and rivers...' and 'Duke Energy's Aquatic Plant Management Program extensively surveys these lakes during the summer months...' None of this body text appears in the HTML.

findings.description: The 'Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers' poster text content from PDF page 6 is not reproduced in the HTML. The text includes the 4-step guidance (CLEAN, DRAIN, DRY, NEVER MOVE) and the reference to www.protectyourwaters.net.
findings.evidence: PDF page 6 contains detailed text: 'Stop the spread of invasive aquatic species. Take these 4 steps after each trip on the water. CLEAN: equipment of aquatic plants and animals. DRAIN: water from boats, live wells, and equipment. DRY: allow equipment to thoroughly dry. NEVER MOVE: fish, plants or other organisms from one body of water to another.' The HTML only has an img tag with alt text 'Stop Aquatic Hitchhikers informational poster'.

findings.description: The SC DNR website content visible in the PDF page 2, including text about South Carolina Noxious Weed Act (Title 46, Chapter 23) and State Crop Pest Act (Title 46, Chapter 9), is not reproduced in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows readable text about 'Some of the Specific State Laws which pertain to Illegal, Noxious, or Nuisance Species' including Title 46, Chapter 23 and Title 46, Chapter 9. The HTML only has an img tag for this content.

findings.description: The 'bad photo' examples (red-bordered) and 'good photo' examples (green-bordered) on page 5 are described with generic alt text that doesn't convey the instructional distinction between good and bad ID photos.
findings.evidence: HTML alt texts: 'Examples of aquatic plants in water and on shore' and 'Close-up examples of individual aquatic plants held in a hand or floating'. The PDF page 5 uses green borders for good examples and red borders for bad examples, which is the key instructional point not captured.
